articles.title: Mexico Exchange Resumes Trading After Hours-Long Halt
articles.summary: Mexico’s BMV Group has resumed trading at the country’s main exchange after an hours-long outage that was its biggest in years.

articles.title: Owners Keep Sending Oil Tankers Through Hormuz After Attacks
articles.summary: Oil tankers continued navigating the Strait of Hormuz, despite a pair of attacks over the past several days that made some owners more cautious about navigating the world’s top maritime chokepoint.

articles.title: France, Oman Back Free Transit Through Hormuz Without Conditions
articles.summary: France’s Emmanuel Macron and Oman’s Sultan Haitham bin Tariq said that they would promote restriction-free transit in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Congo Orders Cobalt Miners to Give Up Unused Export Quotas
articles.summary: Cobalt miners in the Democratic Republic of Congo will be required to surrender unused export quotas for the first half of the year to a government regulator.

articles.title: HDFC Bank Appoints Ex-Bureaucrat Rajiv Kumar as Part-Time Chair

articles.title: Fossil Fuel Subsidies to Hit $1 Trillion in War’s Energy Price Shock, UN Says
articles.summary: Fossil fuel subsidies are projected to reach $1.1 trillion in 2026 as governments spend more to shield consumers from high energy prices triggered by the war in Iran.

articles.title: Siris-Backed TPx Files Bankruptcy, Mulls $1 Billion Debt Cut
articles.summary: Technology solutions provider U.S. TelePacific, also known as TPx Communications, filed for bankruptcy in Texas on Sunday after undergoing repeated debt restructuring while struggling to stay liquid.

articles.title: Tech Giants Power Stock Gains After AI-Fueled Rout: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: A rally in several technology giants lifted stocks as last week’s artificial-intelligence selloff lured buyers betting the industry’s investment boom will continue to support solid corporate earnings.
